title: Olympic medalist Toshiaki Fushimi dies after accident in keirin race
summary: The former silver medalist in Athens 2004 lost his life after falling from his bicycle during a competition at the Matsusaka velodrome at the age of 50.

title: Tree falls on football field in Shizuoka leaves seven injured, including children
summary: A tree about 30 years old collapsed during a match in Gotemba, causing minor injuries to seven people, including minors.

title: Woman dies after being hit by man who fell from apartment building in Nagoya
summary: The 23-year-old victim was walking on the sidewalk when the man fell from an upper floor; the attacker remains in critical condition.

title: Shohei Ohtani delays his next pitching start to prioritize recovery of his left knee
summary: The two-time American League MVP will not pitch on July 22 or 23 and is focused on rehabilitating the injury that prevented him from participating in the All-Star Game.

title: Rintaro Sasaki announces his signing with the Miami Marlins in an emotional press conference
summary: The young Japanese hitter decided to try his luck in the Major Leagues instead of playing in the NPB and assured that he will give his best.

title: Two US military personnel die in Iranian missile attack in Jordan and US bombs targets in Iran
summary: The confrontation escalates with reciprocal attacks for eight consecutive days, while Iran threatens more retaliation.

title: Landslide in Tochigi destroys home and causes death of two elderly people
summary: Heavy rains caused the collapse in Ashikaga City; the bodies of the couple were found under the rubble.

title: Fire ravages historic 'Echigo Tsurukame' sake distillery in Niigata and spreads to homes
summary: The fire burned seven buildings, but no fatalities were reported; authorities are investigating the causes.

title: Japan finishes first round of men's volleyball Nations League undefeated with 12 wins
summary: The Japanese team defeated Argentina 3-0 and qualifies for the final phase of the tournament.

title: Kiev suffers largest ballistic missile attack since start of war with more than 40 projectiles
summary: At least 17 people were killed or wounded in the Russian bombing, which Ukrainian air defenses could not fully stop.

title: Reform of the Imperial Household Law: 60% of Japanese consider that it does not have sufficient public understanding
summary: The government accelerates changes to ensure imperial succession, but public opinion is skeptical and cabinet popularity also falls.

title: Approval of Prime Minister Takaichi's cabinet falls to 41% and disapproval surpasses it for the first time
summary: According to a Mainichi survey, 44% of respondents disapprove of the government, which is a setback for the administration.

title: Japan supplies defense equipment to Cambodia for the first time under Official Security Assistance
summary: The shipment includes surveillance vessels and communication equipment to strengthen maritime security cooperation.

title: Sumo wrestler Kotoeho achieves seven consecutive wins and reaches his best personal ranking
summary: The wrestler, younger brother of ozeki Onosato, stands out for his quick start and solid leg technique.

title: Fire on board a fishing boat off the coast of Miura causes evacuation of eleven people
summary: All occupants were rescued safe and sound, but the vessel sank after the incident.

title: Man injured after being attacked by three bears while inspecting his rice crops
summary: The victim was taken to hospital with abdomen injuries; authorities recommend caution due to the presence of bears.

title: Man arrested for abandoning bodies of two women in mountainous region of Nara
summary: The victims disappeared on the same day and the suspect, an acquaintance of one of them, was arrested on suspicion of homicide.

title: Japan faces extreme heat wave with temperatures up to 38°C in Fukuoka and risk in Saitama
summary: Authorities issue heat stroke warnings for the long weekend, urging the population to take precautions.

title: Shinzo Abe's widow, Akie, expresses confidence in Prime Minister Takaichi to continue her husband's legacy
summary: In a meeting with Deputy Chief Cabinet Secretary Sato, Akie Abe stated that her husband's message was conveyed to the current leader.

title: Argentina and Spain face each other in the World Cup final sixty years after their last match
summary: Defending champion Argentina seeks back-to-back championships, while Spain aspires to its first world title since 2010.
